rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOHN MICHAEL BONJEAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Confirm Ownership Of Estate Property And Authorize Conveyance From Wells Fargo Advisors
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The judicial officer is to review and determine: 1) To the extent the petition requests relief under Probate Code sections 850 and 856 in the form of an order compelling respondent Wells Fargo to perform any act, respondent has not been served with notice with a copy of the petition in the manner required by CCP provisions applicable to service of a civil summons, nor has respondent waived formal service by making a general appearance or otherwise. 2) Are the other two beneficiaries necessary parties who must be joined, in light of the omnibus clause included in the final distribution order arguably shifting standing to sue respondent to each beneficiary from the estate administrator? 3) Should relief against respondent be denied under section 856.5 in favor of a civil action by the beneficiaries as plaintiffs? 4) Should an order merely confirming the subject account as an asset of the estate without compelling respondent to perform any act suffice? PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KENNETH CHARLES KIERNAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The judicial officer is to review and determine: 1) whether the declarations of the decedent's siblings and financial advisor are sufficient to rebut the presumption under Probate Code section 6124 that the decedent destroyed the will with intent to revoke. 2) whether the fact that the original instrument of the devisee trust named in the will cannot be located either remains an obstacle to rebut the section 6124 presumption. 3) whether section 3.03(a) of the devisee trust should be modified to clarify the decedent's intent and remove his directive that the successor trustee must be a private professional fiduciary selected by the trust advisor. It is unclear whether Kathleen Hambly, the decedent's sister and sole trust advisor, knew petitioner was not a private professional fiduciary before exercising her authority given by the devisee trust as trust advisor to nominate petitioner as successor trustee who is entitled to claim appointment as executor of this estate. If the trust modification is necessary: i) Should a new trust case (PTR) be opened under the devisee trust's name with a section 17200 petition to modify an irrevocable trust or to construe ambiguous or conflicting trust provisions? ii) Should Kathleen Hambley, in her trust advisor capacity, be allowed to exercise her authority given by section 3.10(j) of the devisee trust to "correct ambiguities that might otherwise require court construction or reformation", and if so, should the Court treat her nomination of petitioner as trustee on file as modification of the trust under section 3.10(j)? PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RUDOLPH PEDRO MALDONADO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/12/25. Examiner's Notes emailed to attorney with the following issues: 1) Please allege date of surviving spouse's death and whether a personal representative has been appointed over her estate. If no personal representative has been appointed, please specify who the successors in interest are. 2) A written renunciation should be filed by those who are entitled to priority for appointment, but who does not desire to act. Where not filed, the petition should indicate the reason [Local Rule 14.24]. 3) Item 5a(7) is marked but no predeceased issue or issue of predeceased issue is listed in Item 8. 4) Please allege why this estate is being opened at this time as Decedent died in 2010. 5) DE-147 did not include petitioner's phone number. The form must be re-filed. 6) DE-147S has not been filed. 7) Proof of Publication has not been filed.
